shortDescription: Readiness checks for outpatient cardiology referrals driven by symptoms, vitals, ECG data, and active medications.
defaultReferralQuestion: Prepare a cardiology referral packet focused on palpitations and exertional symptoms.

requiredEvidence.label: Clear cardiovascular referral reason
requiredEvidence.description: The chart should explain symptoms or concern that justify specialty review.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Specialists need the triage question up front to route the referral correctly.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Add a concise referral question with symptom duration and main concern.

requiredEvidence.label: Recent vitals or blood pressure data
requiredEvidence.description: Recent BP or vitals should be visible in the chart.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Baseline hemodynamics affect urgency and treatment planning.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Obtain and document repeat vitals before sending the referral.

requiredEvidence.label: ECG or rhythm data
requiredEvidence.description: Recent ECG/rhythm information should be available for palpitations or chest symptoms.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Cardiology triage is materially stronger when recent rhythm data is included.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Attach a recent ECG or rhythm report before sending the referral.

requiredEvidence.label: Active cardiovascular medication list
requiredEvidence.description: Active medications should be available to the specialist.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Medication context changes workup and urgency.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Reconcile active medications before completing the referral.

requiredEvidence.label: Relevant problem list or comorbidities
requiredEvidence.description: Relevant chronic conditions such as hypertension or hyperlipidemia should be present.
requiredEvidence.rationale: Baseline cardiovascular risk factors help frame the specialist question.
requiredEvidence.suggestedAction: Update the problem list with active cardiometabolic conditions.

optionalEvidence.label: Recent lipid data
optionalEvidence.description: Recent lipid panel values strengthen the referral packet.
optionalEvidence.rationale: Useful for outpatient risk stratification but not mandatory for every referral.
optionalEvidence.suggestedAction: Add the most recent lipid panel if available.

redFlags.label: Possible unstable symptoms
redFlags.message: Symptoms may warrant same-day escalation instead of routine referral.

redFlags.label: Possible heart failure symptoms
redFlags.message: Documented symptoms suggest higher-risk cardiology triage.

packetSections.title: Referral Reason
packetSections.promptHint: State the presenting symptoms, duration, and referral objective.

packetSections.title: Pertinent History
packetSections.promptHint: Summarize cardiovascular risk factors, symptoms, and recent vitals.

packetSections.title: Completed Workup
packetSections.promptHint: List ECG data, labs, and chart findings already available.

packetSections.title: Open Gaps Before Visit
packetSections.promptHint: Call out missing information that would strengthen the referral.
